candidates.why: This is a component beneath/within Production Costs, and the selected COGS bucket is the parent total row 'Cost of Revenues/COGS', which matches Production Costs. Adding it separately would double count.
This is also a component beneath/within Production Costs, already embedded in the selected parent COGS total 'Cost of Revenues/COGS'.

variables.notes: Selected COGS row is the reported total 'Cost of Revenues/COGS', and the production D&A rows are clearly sub-components of that total, not separate same-level operating lines to add later.

candidates.why: Appears as a separate D&A line alongside selected SG&A component rows 'Distribution Costs - Other' and 'Administrative Expenses - Other'. Because those selected rows are the '- Other' buckets, this amortization sits outside them and should be added separately. The label appears twice in the statement, once under Distribution Costs and once under Administrative Expenses.
Separate D&A line under the SGA/distribution block, outside the selected '- Other' component row, so it should be added separately.
Separate depreciation line under the administrative SG&A block, outside the selected 'Administrative Expenses - Other' row, so it should be added separately.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket used '- Other' component rows for Distribution and Administrative expenses, which appear to exclude the explicit SGA D&A lines. Therefore the separate SGA D&A rows should be added later. Both occurrences of the exact label 'Amortization in SGA' are included because they correspond to separate distribution and administrative blocks; there is no visible higher-level SGA D&A summary row to prefer instead.
